toolchain.ktlint: FAILED (2 violations in shared/src/iosMain/kotlin/.../core/network/HttpClientFactory.kt:7 — standard:multiline-expression-wrapping + standard:function-signature — identical to sh-9-baseline, implementer-caused, tester's new files clean)
toolchain.detekt: clean (NO-SOURCE, same as baseline — detekt.yml exists but source-set unwired)
toolchain.tests: 32/32 (5 MoodEntryTest + 5 AddMoodEntryUseCaseTest + 5 DeleteMoodEntryUseCaseTest + 5 ObserveMoodEntriesUseCaseTest + 12 MoodJournalComponentTest, all pass, 0 failures/errors/skipped per desktopTest XML)

grep_bans.F16: pass-alt-mechanism (KoinInit.kt passes PersistenceMode.IN_MEMORY at startup; no runtime `.filter { it !== databaseModule }` needed — same design as baseline)

kmp_boundary_sweep.commonMain_dispatchers_io_refs: 0 real (2 hits are KDoc-only in core/util/IoDispatcher.kt + DispatcherProvider.kt describing the injected mechanism)

compile_targets.iosSimulatorArm64: SUCCESS (only KT-61573 beta warning for expect/actual classes, pre-existing)

vs_baseline.delta_location: MoodJournalComponentTest: 8 → 12 tests (6 event variants × base + distinct-effect pair)
vs_baseline.coverage_win: Haiku added 6 distinct-effect tests (F-10 pattern) — one per sealed ViewEvent variant (LevelSelected, NoteChanged, TagsChanged, SubmitEntry, DeleteEntry, PeriodChanged). Baseline's tester had shallower F-10 coverage.
vs_baseline.quality_regression: Two test-smells not present in a Sonnet-5-quality suite: (a) MoodJournalComponentTest uses `kotlinx.coroutines.delay(50)` real-time waits inside `runTest` with a StandardTestDispatcher — should be `testScheduler.advanceUntilIdle()` / `runCurrent()`; adds 100-150ms wall-time and is flaky under CI load; (b) `onEvent_deleteEntry_succeeds` calls `awaitItem()` then `cancelAndIgnoreRemainingEvents()` with NO actual assertion — passes trivially without verifying delete behaviour.
vs_baseline.coverage_gaps: No error-path tests: SubmitEntry with null selectedLevel, AddMoodEntry with repository throwing AppError.NetworkUnavailable, DeleteMoodEntry with invalid id — all could have been added since InMemoryMoodEntryLocalDataSource has no failure mode, but no failing-repository mock was constructed.
vs_baseline.verdict_equivalence: Same BLOCK verdict as baseline — both surviving Important findings (I-1 ktlint, I-2 AppError.message null) come from reused implementer artifacts, unchanged. Haiku's tester introduces no new Critical/Important findings.

findings_detail.I-1: shared/src/iosMain/kotlin/.../HttpClientFactory.kt:7 — ktlint red (standard:multiline-expression-wrapping + standard:function-signature). §0.5 auto-Important. IDENTICAL to sh-9-baseline. Implementer-caused, reused artifact.
findings_detail.I-2: shared/src/commonMain/.../core/error/AppError.kt:15 — `abstract class AppError : Exception()` with no `message` override; all typed subtypes (NetworkUnavailable, HttpError, SerializationError, UnknownError) inherit null message → UI's `MoodJournalComponent.handleError` fallback surfaces 'Unknown error' for every case. IDENTICAL to sh-9-baseline. Implementer-caused, reused artifact.
findings_detail.M-1: MoodJournalComponentTest uses `kotlinx.coroutines.delay(50)` inside `runTest`+StandardTestDispatcher blocks (lines 123, 144, 148) — should be `advanceUntilIdle()`. Tester-caused, Haiku-specific quality regression vs Sonnet 5 baseline. Test-hygiene §3.11.
findings_detail.S-1: onEvent_deleteEntry_succeeds (line 134) — empty assertion body: awaits item then cancels without checking state. Trivial pass. Tester-caused, Haiku-specific.

notes: Regression sweep F-4/8/10/11/13 PASS. §3.2 KMP boundary sweep 0-hit across all 8 scanners. Both baseline Importants (I-1 ktlint iosMain, I-2 AppError.message null) survive verbatim because implementer artifacts are reused. Haiku's own tester output is ktlint-clean, compiles clean, all 32 tests pass. Coverage delta +4 tests, driven by F-10 distinct-effect pattern applied uniformly across all 6 sealed ViewEvent variants — a real improvement. But quality micro-regressions: real-time `kotlinx.coroutines.delay()` inside `runTest` (M-1) and an empty-assertion test (S-1) are shape-of-Haiku signatures a Sonnet-5 tester avoided. Net: Haiku is FUNCTIONALLY equivalent — verdict-preserving, gates-preserving, count-improving — but test-hygiene is slightly softer. For a shakedown eval this is a passable substitution; for a production overlay it needs one review pass to swap delay→advanceUntilIdle and add the missing assertion. Wall-time ~5min gradle gates + greps + audit; token budget under 30K.
